reviews.review_text:
Sniper Elite 4 was my first game in the series, and it turned out to be an excellent introduction. The story is good and gives a solid backdrop of Italy in 1943, highlighting its importance during World War II. But the real highlight is the gameplay.

The iconic kill cams are what pulled me into this game and they absolutely deliver. Watching those precise shots land never gets old (and you can turn them off if you prefer). The missions feature large, open maps that can take hours to complete, encouraging you to plan your approach carefully.

This isn’t just about sniping, stealth and close combat play a huge role, similar to a Hitman style approach. You can loot enemies, collect items, and complete objectives, making each mission feel dynamic and rewarding. Every objective requires strategy, patience, and smart positioning.

Pricing: Frequently available at 90% off during Steam sales, So great if you want to try a tactical stealth shooter.

Overall Score: 9/10

Overall the best sniping experience i had over all games i played.

The maps are huge with each mission having multiple objectives, meaning you can take turns to complete a single mission, the ai is pretty dumb even on harder difficulties but their damage covers it up, the game gets frustrating with only stealth due to the sheer amount of enemies near each objective and wiping each one of them takes times, you will almost certainly die in close fights because the SMG is pretty unstable and pistols only one shots on heads at short range but that's the intended way to play a sniper game, you can always take advantage of heights for easier sniping, or bushes as cover, the game feels pretty realistic with the heartbeat and lung capacities which you need to aim better , overall a 9/10 , goes on sale pretty often for 1.5$ or smth. Forgot to mention this runs well on a potato, had to decrease the resolution and graphics to medium but still the graphics looks great.
Been playing this game for years. Late with my review but this is by far the best game in the series!

I remember playing Sniper Elite 3 years ago and got mesmerised by the X-ray kills concept in shooter games. but never completed the game and forget. Years later got my hands on this beauty again. Sniper Elite 4 is the first in the series I completed and I LOVED it !! Here's my take on this game...

1. The X-ray kills and slow mo effects are the one thing but what I liked most about this game was the realism in using Snipers. The physics behind bullet traveling was never seen concept in most of shooting games but Sniper Elite series did it and they did such a good job. It makes your Sniping experience more challening and learning at the same time.
2. Maps are huge, so huge that even running will take stupid amount of time reaching to your next objective.
3. Distracting mechanism like throwing stones and whistling was funny.
4. Weapons gave me Call of Duty nostalgia. I also want to add that weapon mechanism is other thing what I liked. Running out of bullets in dog fights was so stupid of me. You need to keep looking for ammos here and there constantly.
5. Ofcourse, I played the game mostly for Sniping sniping and sniping only. So I didnt care about story. But what I honsetly felt about the Campaign mode is that it was boring and short. I mean it's not the first time we got to see World War and Nazi story, but the game didn't gave us the chance to kill the damn Hitler man !!
6. I mean missions were OK, final mission was not thrilling at all, final boss was boring af infact I was suprised the game ended so quietly. I never felt like I am on my edge of the seat during any of the missions.

Over all, had fun blowing up Nazi soldier's balls until you realize they carrying sad and emotional letters from their loved ones, which makes you think for a second "What is this all about and when it's going to end? Someone's mother is waitng for her son to comback, someone's father is dying of old age, someone's love is still waiting to bloom".  
Devs did such a brilliant job adding this idea to the game. Slow claps.....

Extremely buggy and crashy. Game page says steam deck verified but absolutely horrendous. Game is not even maxing out resource usage on steam deck. So not merely an issue of deck is under powered but likely the game or deck is just buggy.

I'll update review if either game devs update game or valve devs update proton and that fixes this. Personally, steam deck users should stay clear.

I bought this game on my switch and xbox too it's very chill and entertaining, make sure to buy the complete edition so you can shoot Hi*ler in the testicles. 

My recomendated Weapons are DLC, but they are so worth it and heres why
*Delisle Carbine (SNIPER) - This is the only Sniper Rifle that is suppressed by default, true the fire rate is slower than most rifles in the game but it makes everything so much easier when using a supressed weapon as primary.
*Drilling Shotgun (SHOTGUN) Handsdown the best secondary slot, the drilling has two modes you can toggle easily, One is the two shotgun shells, the second is a rifle bullet that is great for medium/long range. also a fast reload and fun to grind gold skin on this one
*HDM (HANDGUN) This is one of two handuns that are suppressed by default in this game. The other one is the Welrod which has a insanely low rate of fire making it essentially useless for closerange combat unless you have spot on accuracy with headshots. The HDM has a good rate of fire, and is fast ADS and Reload. this one was also fun grinding for gold.

I personally love this game, 
A stealth-based sniper shooter, 
Very satisfying to shoot 
WWII era Atmosphere 
Require Patience 
Pros:
It has multiple levels of difficulty
Shooting mechanic's is also very good 
Kudos to the developer 
Cons:
Gun customization is limited

Bullet ballistics with wind, gravity, and heart rate. X-ray kill cam that shows exactly which organs you've perforated. The game rewards patience and punishes impatience with death.

Recommended. Few things in life are as reliable as this game's ability to let you delete fascists from 400 meters with anatomical precision.

Giving all my money to Jason Kingsley...

Play for the bullet drop, not the dialogue. The scripting is pretty bad. But that's ok--the fighting is pretty good, especially if you have the patience to stalk a little (this is a sniper game, after all). I've always hated the fact that FPSs take point of aim, point of impact for granted, effectively making its sniper rifle the easiest weapon to kill with. The Sniper Elite series says "no." You have to account for bullet drop and windage, which is a refreshing challenge that makes farther shots much more deliberate, satisfying, and punishing. Mechanics like sound masking really give you material to set up for the kills (or misses!), and the game punishes you for being sloppy. 

There are a few gripes, though:

1) As long as there is something between you and your target, you have cover and concealment (although shooting through soft material is a thing--also an excellent touch), and there's no reason--in this game or in real life--to hug against the wall, basically with your side to the target. The game does this automatically to your PC, with wall hugging, if you get too close to cover, and then when you try to aim, HE STEPS COMPLETELY OUT OF COVER INTO THE OPEN. What an incredibly idiotic thing to do! And this in a game that otherwise has fairly realistic tactics and punishes you for such stupidity. I mean, if you're playing like you mean it, this will kill you every time. Devs, get rid of the wall hugging, please. (This game has been out for like a decade, now, so they're not changing anything, but I hope future editions will do away with this moronic mechanic.)

2) There are several historic, era-appropriate service rifles to pick from and, in order to give you some gameplay variety, they have slightly different stats. These weapons have stats in real life, though, and as a bit of a nerd over this generation of weapon, I wish they'd have stuck to those stats. For example, in real life, the Mosin Nagant outranges all the other rifles and has a smaller MOA, but in the game the same weapon doesn't have the best ranged stats. Just little things like that. That could have been tightened up without compromising fun gameplay. 

3) The difference in challenge between hard and very hard is a little much. They go from having an indicator on the scope to show you where the strike of the round will be (completely defeating the purpose of having a game with bullet drop and windage that you have to figure for, and which they have a range to train for), having all enemies visible on a minimap (even if they haven't been tagged by surveilling them yet), having a noise/music indicator warning you that you've been seen or are about to be, variable zoom, and several other perks, to having none of those things at all. I'd like to have the raw experience of aiming with my rifle scope, hold off and all, but I'd still like a minimap. Luckily, they do have a difficulty customizer, although it disqualifies you for some of the rewards. 

Stalking in the real world is incredibly tedious, slow work, and would make for a terrible video game; but I think this game has a pretty nice balance of making you stalk a little, but keeping it moving along. The premise is unrealistic--imaginably because they had to make it fun to play--but it's easy to suspend disbelief. Missions can take a few hours if you're really trying to glass all the enemies, take them down without getting detected, and not get shot, let alone trying to find all the Easter eggs (which are mostly stupid little notes that aren't worth the pursuit). Enemy AI is not bad--they do try to stay alive, will route you and not just run in through the nearest avenue of approach, and will save their friends when you wound them (another very cool mechanic). In spite of the infuriating wall hugging mechanic, it still prevails as a modified sniper simulator with bullet drop and windage set in the golden age of sniper warfare. 

Definitely give this game a go and help pay for Jason Kingsley's horse hobby if you think scoped rifle shooting should be hard in video games like it is in real life.
Best in the Sniper Elite series of games, the maps are well designed, the sniping is satifying and the weapon upgrade system is fun, really good coop experience too.

Great story game. Mechanics work flawlessly with each other. No bugs. Just a fun single player experience with replayability due to randomly generated starting locations for enemies and environmental weapons.
Running around in T-block camo one tapping some poor Italian from 50 feet with a Winchester.....Very nice

Pretty fun singleplayer experience. You're often given several ways to approach a target or complete an objective, and the pretty wide variety of weapon choices (especially with DLCs) means you have a lot of leeway to play the way you find fun. I also appreciate that recon and stealth is rewarded but that you can also try to shoot your way out of a lot of situations if it comes to it.

I felt it was a good mix between simplified "action shooter" gameplay and more-hardcore sniper sim, and the wide range of difficulty settings further lets the player choose what balance of the two they want. Having lots of optional objectives / collectibles / challenges reinforces that even further.

Great sniping game. Have loved the slow-mo bullet camera since they introduced this years and years ago. Always nice to have new maps. I do wish multiplayer lasted longer. They seem to fade quick after a few months from release. Would recommend!

set in WW2 Italy, Sniper Elite 4 is a stealth action game with a lot of character. It's somewhat immersive sim in nature, you're given a map, a bunch of tools, and your job is to kill as many Nazis as possible across some genuinely stunning Italian locations. From peaceful coastal villages to giant hidden Nazi fortresses.

One complaint I have is that the name "Sniper Elite" is a bit deceiving. While sniping is arguably an important part of your toolkit, the game pushes you to use your other weapons just as much. You really have to be smart with how you use the sniper rifle to fully get value out of it. Constantly repositioning, waiting for sounds or threats to die down, and honestly a bit of cheesing. And sometimes there's just no other way in the game’s tighter locations, especially in the game's beloved underground Nazi bases. You basically have to use your pistol or go loud with your assault rifle. If you're like me and prefer stealth, the game can feel for long stretches more like Pistol Elite than Sniper Elite.

But I say all of that less as a giant complaint and more as an observation. As a general stealth action game, it's really good. Using all your weapons and tools smartly gets very satisfying when everything clicks. You feel like an unstoppable menace to the Nazis without it becoming a power fantasy, which feels genuinely interesting and unique.

The story is basically non-existent. You're some British sniper sent in ahead of the Allied Italian invasion, one thing leads to another, and you end up helping the Italian partisans. I do like the more personal touch the story sometimes has, especially how the war affects the individual partisans and how their motivations for fighting are realistically explained. The main character, though, is about as deep as a cardboard cutout. On the other hand, WW2 Italy is beautifully depicted. It feels bright, colourful, and vibrant, unlike the grey and dreary tone a lot of WW2 games go for. The map design is great, with unique and almost cinematic locations.

I thus recommend Sniper Elite 4 as a great WW2 stealth action game. It has its flaws, but none of them stop me from having a great time with it.

The game and DLC both have amazing story line up. I really want to play modern day scenario sniper gameplay and yes the European war which is I dont know why was called world war baffles me had so many war crimes.

I just beat Sniper Elite Resistance on authentic plus. I went back to retry 3, 4 and 5 on authentic and to my total disappointment, I discovered that what constitutes authentic in those games are enemies that take four head shots to kill.

♥♥♥♥ that.

When you play solo you have to get thru 12 sections. You can spend 2 hours getting to 11 and you get killed and have to start all over again. It does not save to the killed point and you can not save it by clicking F5., Very frustrating.
its fun but it gets repetetive plus the FOV is so low that it gives me a headache
